questions.question: Find the length of chord which is at a distance of 12 cm from the centre of the circle of diameter 30 cm.
questions.explanation: Let AB is the chord and O is the centre of circle. OM = 12 cm and OB = 15 cm (Given) In △BOM, BM2 = OB2 - OM2 = 152 - 122 = 81 => BM = 9 cm Length of chord AB = 2 * BM = 2 * 9 = 18 cm

questions.question: x4 -2x3 + 3x2 -4x -4 is divisible by
questions.explanation: From option (1) x4 -2x3 + 3x2 -4x -4 is divisible by x-2 if it gives 0 for x =2 x4 -2x3 + 3x2 -4x -4 = (2)4 -2(2)3 + 3(2)2 -4(2) -4 = 16 -16 +12-8 -4 =0 So, option (1) is the answer.

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys some rice for Rs 5400. He sells one third of it at a loss of 5%. At what percent gain should he sell the remaining rice so as to make an overall profit of 15% on the whole transaction?
questions.explanation: Total SP = 5400 x 1.15 = Rs 6210 SP of 1/3rd of rice = 1800 x 0.95 = Rs 1710 CP of remaining rice = 5400 x 2/3 = Rs 3600 SP of remaining rice = 6210-1710 = Rs 4500 Profit % = (4500-3600)/3600 x 100 = 25%

questions.question: A shopkeeper buys a TV set priced as Rs.20000 after getting successive discounts of 15% and 20%. He spends 8% of his cost price on packaging and transport. At what price should he sell the TV set to earn a profit of 12%?
questions.explanation: Net price after two successive discounts = 20000 x 0.85 x 0.80 = 13600 Net cost price = 13600 x 1.08 = 14688 Selling price = 14688 x 1.12 = 16450.56

questions.question: In the following figure m||n||p. Find the value of B in terms of A.
questions.explanation: In triangle SQU, ∠Q = A, ∠S = 90. Hence ∠SUQ = 90-A ∠QUV = 180 - ∠SUQ = 90+A (Linear pair) Now, n||p. So ∠B = 90+A

questions.question: What must be added to each terms of the ratio 2: 18 so that it may equal to 3:11 ?
questions.explanation: Let the number to be added be a Hence, (2+a)/(18+a) = (3/11) Or, 22 + 11a = 54 + 3a Or, 8a = 32 Or, a = 4

questions.question: If 3cos2A+7sin2A=4 then find value of cot A, given that A is an acute angle?
questions.explanation: 3cos2A+7sin2A=4 Or, 3cos2A+3sin2A+4sin2A =4 ; Or, 3(cos2A+sin2A) +4sin2A =4 Or, 3(1)+ 4sin2A =4 Or, 4sin2A=4-3 Or, Sin2A=1/4 Or, sinA=√1/4= 1/2 So, A = 30° Thus, cot 30°= √3.

questions.question: A copper-wire when bent in the form of a square enclose an area of 121 cm2. If the same wire is bent in the form of a circle, then the area of the circle is
questions.explanation: Side of square = √121 = 11cm Length of wire = circumference of circle. 44 = 2 π r or r = (44 x 7)/(2 x 22) = 7 cm Hence, area of circle = pr2 = 22/7 x 7 x 7 = 154 cm2

questions.question: If (x - a) (x - b) = 1 and a - b + 4 = 0, what is the value of (x - a)² + 1/(x - a)²?
If cos θ = A, then find: cos θ_________1 - sin θ - tan θ
questions.explanation: cos θ/(1- sin θ) - sin θ/cos θ = cos 2θ - sin θ (1 - sin θ)/ ((1- sin θ) cos θ) = (cos 2θ + sin 2θ - sin θ)/((1- sin θ) cos θ) = 1 / cos θ = 1/A

questions.question: The least number which when divided by 44 leaves a remainder 38, when divided by 55 leaves the remainder 49 and when divided by 64 leaves 58 is
The numerical value of 1 + 1/tan²36 - cosec²54 + 1/cos²36 - sec²54 is
questions.explanation: 1 + cot²36 - cosec²54 + sec²36 - sec²54 cosec²36 - cosec²54 + cosec²54 - cosec²36 = 0

questions.question: Gas bill paid by Mohit is how much percent lower than the gas bill paid by Mohan for the given period?
questions.explanation: Option 2 Gas bill paid by Mohit for the given period = 3287 Gas bill paid by Mohan for the given period = 3695 Required percentage = [(3695-3287)/3695]*100 = 11.04%

questions.question: What is the difference between the amount paid by Manoj in the month of March and May on utility bills?
questions.explanation: Option 4 Amount paid by Manoj in the month of March on utility bills = 1771 Amount paid by Manoj in the month of May on utility bills = 1824 Required difference = 53

questions.question: In which month is the percent change in the electricity bill paid by Manoj the second highest over previous month?
In which month telephone bill paid by all the three person is second highest?
questions.explanation: Option 3 Telephone bill paid by all the three person in January = 1568 Telephone bill paid by all the three person February = 1610 Telephone bill paid by all the three person March = 1566 Telephone bill paid by all the three person April = 1796 Telephone bill paid by all the three person May = 1881

questions.question: A can complete a work in 10 days. B and C respectively are 40% and 50% as efficient as A. B and C started the work and worked for 5 days. After that A finished the remaining work by himself. In how many days did the work get over?
questions.explanation: Work done by A in a day = 1/10 Work done by B in a day = 1/10 x 4/10 = 1/25 Work done by C in a day = 1/10 x 5/10 = 1/20 Work done by B and C in 5 days = 5 x (1/25 + 1/20) = 9/20 Remaining work = 11/20 Time taken by A to finish the remaining work = 11/20 x 10 = 11/2 days Total time = 5 + 11/2 = 21/2 = 10.5 days

questions.question: In a quadrilateral ABCD, AB = BC = 25 cm, CD = 20 cm and ∠D = 90°. If diagonal AC = 25 cm, find the area of the quadrilateral.
questions.explanation: Area of the ABCD = Area of ∆ACD + Area of ∆ABC By applying Pythagoras theorem, AD = √(625-400) = 15 cm Area of ∆ACD = ½ x 15 x 20 = 150 sq. cm Area of ∆ABC = √3/4 x 252 = 0.433 x 625 = 270.625 sq.cm Therefore, area of quadrilateral ABCD = 420.63 sq cm

questions.question: If a+ 1/a +2=0, then find the value of a36 -1/a49.
In the given fig., EF=EG. Find x.
The average age of a family of five members is 24. If the present age of the youngest member is 8 years, what was the average age of the family at the time of the birth of the youngest member?
questions.explanation: Total age of five member of a family = 24*5 = 120 Total age of the four members at the time of the birth of the youngest member = 120 - 8*5 = 80yr Hence, required average age = 80/4 = 20yrs

questions.question: A sum was given at simple interest at a certain rate for 3 years. Had it been given at 2% higher rate, it would have fetched Rs.420 more. Find the sum
questions.explanation: Let sum = P and original rate = R. Then, [(P X (R+2)X 3)/100]- [(P X R X 3)/100]=420 => 3PR + 6P - 3PR = 42000 => 6P = 42000 => P = 7000

questions.question: 4 kg of alloy contains ½ part of lead and the rest is tin. Another 5 kg of alloy contains 1/6 part of lead and the rest is tin. The ratio of lead and tin into the mixture of these two alloys:
questions.explanation: Lead in 4 kg = 2 kg Tin in 4 kg = 2 kg Lead in 5 kg = 5/6 kg Tin in 5 kg = 25/6 kg Lead in mixture = 2+5/6 = 17/6 Tin in mixture = 2+25/6 = 37/6 Required ratio = 17:37

questions.question: A motorboat can travel at 10 km/hr in still water. It travelled 91 kms downstream in a river and then returned to the same place, taking altogether 20 hrs. the rate of flow of river is?
questions.explanation: Speed of boat = 10 km/h Let the speed of flow of river = x km/h Upstream speed = (10-x) km/h Downstream speed = (10+x) km/h So, 91/(10-x) + 91/(10+x) = 20 91 = 100 - x2 x2 = 9 x = 3 km/hr

questions.question: If 45% of(x - y) = 30% of(x + y), then what percent of x is y?
questions.explanation: 45% of (x - y) = 30% of (x + y) => 45/100 (x-y)= 30/100(x+y) =>3(x - y) = 2(x + y) =>3x - 3y = 2x + 2y =>x = 5y Let A% of x = y Required percentage = (y/x X 100)% = (y/5y X 100)% = 20 %
